title: Legends of the Gods The Egyptian Texts, edited with Translations

memes.label: "LEGENDS OF THE GODS"
memes.description: "LEGENDS OF THE GODS"

memes.label: Abydos
memes.description: The ancient Egyptian city of Abtu (Greek Abydos), the most sacred cult center of Osiris, site of the annual commemoration festival and pilgrimage destination where the head of Osiris was believed to be enshrined.

memes.label: Amen
memes.description: Amen (Amun, "the Hidden One") is the great god of Thebes, a primordial creator deity later merged with Ra as Amen-Ra, called "Father Amen, Lord of the thrones of the Two Lands."

memes.label: Anubis
memes.description: Jackal-headed god of embalming and the dead, guardian of tombs and the necropolis, who presided over mummification and the weighing of the heart.

memes.label: Apep
memes.description: The great serpent of chaos and chief enemy of Ra; personification of darkness who attempted to prevent the sun-god from rising each morning.

memes.label: Apis
memes.description: The sacred bull-god of Memphis, living incarnation of Ptah and later of Osiris, whose cult was among the most enduring and widely attested in ancient Egypt.

memes.label: Ashtoreth
memes.description: Semitic war goddess imported into Egypt, depicted driving a chariot over defeated enemies and accompanying Ra into battle against the foes of Horus.

memes.label: Battle of Horus and Set
memes.description: The cosmic battle between Horus (son of Isis) and Set (murderer of Osiris) for the throne of Egypt, culminating in the defeat and beheading of Set and the establishment of Horus as the legitimate king.

memes.label: Bekhten
memes.description: A foreign kingdom whose prince sought Egyptian divine intervention when his daughter Bent-enth-resh was possessed by a spirit, leading to the miraculous journey of the god Khensu Pa-ari-sekher from Thebes.

memes.label: Bennu
memes.description: The Bennu is the divine bird of Egyptian mythology — the soul of Ra, the incarnation of Osiris, and the keeper of the book of destiny — identified with the Phoenix of classical tradition.

memes.label: Birth of Horus
memes.description: Isis gives birth to Horus, son of Osiris, in the papyrus swamps of the Delta, hiding him from Set until he grows strong enough to avenge his father.

memes.label: Boat of Ra
memes.description: The divine solar barque in which the sun god Ra traverses the sky by day and the underworld by night, crewed by gods and protected from the serpent Apep.

memes.label: Book of the Dead
memes.description: The ancient Egyptian funerary text known as the Book of the Dead — a collection of spells, hymns, and instructions for the deceased to navigate the afterlife and attain eternal life.
aliases: Chapters of Coming Forth by Day
Book of Coming Forth by Day

memes.label: Burial of Osiris's Pieces
memes.description: After Set dismembers Osiris's body into fourteen pieces, Isis travels Egypt burying each piece where found, giving rise to the many sepulchres of Osiris throughout the country.

memes.label: Busiris
memes.description: The ancient Egyptian city of Pa-Asar (Greek Busiris), a primary cult center of Osiris in the Delta, where the backbone of Osiris was believed to be enshrined, and home to distinctive Isis-related rituals.

memes.label: Buto
memes.description: An ancient Delta city, cult center of the cobra goddess Wadjet, known as the marshy refuge where Isis hid the infant Horus from Set and where the divine nurses tended him.

memes.label: Byblos
memes.description: A Phoenician port city on the Mediterranean coast where the chest containing the body of Osiris drifted ashore and lodged in a cedar tree, leading Isis to journey there to retrieve it.

memes.label: Coronation of Osiris
memes.description: Osiris is crowned king of Egypt at Suten-henen, inheriting the earth from his father Keb and the sovereignty of heaven from his mother Nut.

memes.label: Council of the Gods
memes.description: Ra assembles the divine council in the Great House of Heliopolis to consult the gods on how to deal with mankind's rebellion, resulting in the sending of Hathor as the Eye of Ra.

memes.label: Creation Myth
memes.description: The Egyptian creation myth as narrated by the god Neb-er-tcher, describing the emergence of the cosmos from primeval matter through the god Khepera.

memes.label: Death of Horus by a Scorpion
memes.description: The mythological event in which the child Horus died from the sting of the scorpion Uhat, sent by Set, and was resurrected through the magical words of power spoken by Thoth at the entreaty of Isis.
aliases: Legend of the Death and Resurrection of Horus

memes.label: Destruction of Mankind
memes.description: The mythological event in which mankind rebelled against the aged sun god Ra, prompting divine retribution through the goddess Hathor and the subsequent salvation of humanity through a trick with dyed beer.

memes.label: Diospolis Parva
memes.description: A city in the Nile Delta nome, known as Sma-Behutet ("Thebes of the North"), where the Nile was measured at seven cubits and where Isis buried one of the pieces of Osiris.

memes.label: Divine Company
memes.description: The assembly of gods who convened in Heliopolis to acknowledge the sovereignty of the dead king and participate in divine judgment.

memes.label: Divine Statue
memes.description: The Egyptian theological concept of divine indwelling in cult statues, wherein the image became a living vessel of the god through the transfer of sa (magical life-force).

memes.label: Edfu
memes.description: The ancient Egyptian city of Edfu (Behutet), cult center of Horus of Behutet, site of the great Temple of Horus and the scene of the Winged Disk myth.

memes.label: Elephantine
memes.description: The island city of Abu (Greek Elephantine) at the First Cataract of the Nile, cult center of the ram-headed god Khnemu, regulator of the Nile flood, and southern frontier of ancient Egypt.

memes.label: Elysian Fields
memes.description: The blessed afterlife realm (Sekhet-hetep or Sekhet Aaru) where the righteous dead dwelled in eternal peace and abundance, the Egyptian equivalent of paradise.

memes.label: Festivals
memes.description: The sacred festivals and processions of ancient Egypt, celebrating the gods' deeds through ritual drama, feasting, and public worship throughout the year.

memes.label: Hapi
memes.description: God of the Nile and its annual inundation; personification of the life-giving flood that sustained Egyptian agriculture.

memes.label: Hathor
memes.description: Hathor (Het-hert, "House of Horus") is the Egyptian sky goddess who manifests as the fiery Eye of Ra sent to destroy mankind, later pacified through beer and transformed into a goddess of love, music, and festivity.

memes.label: Heben
memes.description: A city in the Edfu region, the site of a river-bank battle between Horus of Heben and the followers of Set, and the place where a priest established an annual commemoration of Horus's victory.

memes.label: Heh
memes.description: Primordial god of infinity and eternity, member of the Ogdoad of Hermopolis, who personifies the boundless expanse of the primeval waters and the unending span of time.

memes.label: Heka
memes.description: Heka is the Egyptian god of magic and "words of power," the personification of the divine creative energy (heka) through which the gods perform their will and mortals obtain protection in life and after death.

memes.label: Heliopolis
memes.description: The ancient Egyptian city of Annu (Greek Heliopolis), the primary cult center of the sun god Ra and seat of the Ennead theology, located near modern Cairo.

memes.label: Hermopolis
memes.description: The Egyptian city of Khemenu (Greek Hermopolis), principal cult center of Thoth, the Twice-Great god of wisdom and writing, and one of the greatest religious centers of ancient Egypt.

memes.label: Heru
memes.description: Falcon-headed sky god; Heru-ur (Horus the Elder) is the cosmic falcon who defends Ra and battles Set in the form of the Winged Disk.

memes.label: Heru-Behutet
memes.description: Heru-Behutet is the winged sun-disk form of Horus, the divine warrior who fought alongside Ra against Set and the rebel forces, commemorated at Edfu.

memes.label: Horus
memes.description: The falcon-headed sky god, son of Osiris and Isis, avenger of his father, and the divine prototype of Egyptian kingship.

memes.label: Isis
memes.description: Great goddess of magic, motherhood, and protection; wife of Osiris and mother of Horus.

memes.label: Isis and the Seven Scorpions
memes.description: Isis escapes captivity aided by Seven Scorpion-goddesses; after a woman refuses her shelter the scorpions avenge her, but Isis compassionately heals the child; later Horus is stung by the scorpion Uhat and Thoth restores him to life.

memes.label: Judgment of Horus in Anu
memes.description: Thoth takes charge of Horus's case in the Judgment Hall of Anu where Osiris was judged, advocating for Horus's rightful succession against all accusations.

memes.label: Khensu
memes.description: Khensu is the Theban moon-god renowned for his healing and protective powers, whose statue was sent to a foreign princess to expel a possessing spirit in the Legend of the Possessed Princess of Bekhten.

memes.label: Khensu and the Princess of Bekhten
memes.description: The legend of the statue of Khensu Pa-ari-sekher travelling from Thebes to Bekhten to exorcise an evil spirit from the Princess Bent-enth-resh in the reign of Rameses II.

memes.label: Khepera
memes.description: Khepera is the scarab beetle form of the Egyptian sun god, representing the sun at dawn and the principle of self-creation, called "Khepera in the morning" in the solar cycle triad.

memes.label: Khnemu
memes.description: Khnemu (Khnum) is the creator-god of the First Cataract, the divine potter who moulds humankind on his wheel and controls the annual inundation of the Nile from his cavern at Elephantine.

memes.label: Koptos
memes.description: An Upper Egyptian city, cult center of the god Menu (Min), whose name was reinterpreted by Plutarch as the "city of mourning" after Isis buried one of Osiris's dismembered body parts there.

memes.label: Legend of Ra and Isis
memes.description: The myth in which Isis created a sacred serpent from Ra's spittle, caused it to bite the aging Sun-god, and obtained his secret name in exchange for healing — gaining supreme magical power over the creator of the universe.

memes.label: Maakheru
memes.description: The ancient Egyptian formula of justification, "true of voice," declaring the deceased righteous in the judgment hall of Osiris and granting divine authority over gods, spirits, and fiends.

memes.label: Maat
memes.description: Goddess of truth, law, justice, and cosmic order; female counterpart of Thoth who assisted Khepera in laying the foundation of creation.

memes.label: Memphis
memes.description: The ancient Egyptian city of Men-nefer (Greek Memphis), the traditional capital of unified Egypt and primary cult center of Ptah and the Apis bull, located near modern Cairo.

memes.label: Menthu
memes.description: The war-god of Thebes, the "mighty one of battle," whose strength was invoked in royal titulary and whose divine limbs were called upon in magical healing spells.

memes.label: Mesent
memes.description: A double town in the Edfu legend, comprising Mesent of the West and Mesent of the East, the domain of the blacksmiths of Heru-Behutet, also called Kau-Mesent.

memes.label: Murder of Osiris by Set
memes.description: Set murders his brother Osiris during a war, hurls his body to the ground at Netat near Abydos, and usurps the throne of Egypt.

memes.label: Neb-Er-Tcher
memes.description: Neb-Er-Tcher, the "Lord of the Universe," is the primordial creator-god of the Heliopolitan cosmogony who preceded all forms and brought creation into being by speaking words of power, later manifesting as Khepera.

memes.label: Nekhebet
memes.description: Vulture-goddess of Upper Egypt, protector of the pharaoh and of mothers in childbirth, paired with Uatchit as the Two Ladies.

memes.label: Nephthys
memes.description: Nephthys (Nebt-het, "Lady of the House") is the funerary goddess of the Heliopolitan Ennead, daughter of Geb and Nut, sister of Isis, Osiris, and Set, who mourns and protects the dead alongside Isis.

memes.label: Neter
memes.description: The ancient Egyptian term "neter" (plural "neteru"), denoting a god or divine force — a fundamental theological concept referring to the supernatural powers that govern the cosmos.

memes.label: Nu
memes.description: Nu is the personification of the primordial World-Ocean—the vast, inert watery abyss from which the creator-god first emerged and the source of the celestial Nile.

memes.label: Nubia
memes.description: The region south of Egypt, known to Egyptians as Uaua or Ta-Kens, famed for its archers ("Land of the Bow") and the site of Ra-Harmakhis's military campaigns in the 363rd year of his reign.

memes.label: Nut
memes.description: Nut is the sky-goddess of the Heliopolitan Ennead, the mother of Osiris, Isis, Set and Nephthys, who arches over the earth as the celestial vault and swallowed the sun each evening to rebirth it at dawn.

memes.label: Offerings
memes.description: The practice of presenting offerings — food, drink, incense, and sacrificial animals — to the Egyptian gods as an act of worship, propitiation, and cosmic maintenance.

memes.label: Osiris
memes.description: God of the dead, resurrection, and kingship; murdered by Set and revived by Isis, ruler of the Underworld.

memes.label: Overthrow of Apep
memes.description: The daily ritual battle in which Ra overthrows the serpent Apep in the Underworld, ensuring the sun's continued journey across the sky.

memes.label: Oxyrhynchus
memes.description: An Upper Egyptian city named after the Oxyrhynchus fish, which was venerated and tabooed because it was believed to have consumed the phallus of Osiris after his dismemberment by Set.

memes.label: Philae
memes.description: A sacred Nile island at the traditional boundary of Egypt and Nubia, site of the great Temple of Isis where the goddess was worshipped until the reign of the Byzantine emperor Justinian.
aliases: Island of the Time of Ra

memes.label: Plutarch
memes.description: Plutarch of Chaeronea (c. 46–120 CE) was a Greek biographer and priest whose treatise De Iside et Osiride serves as a primary source for the Egyptian Osiris myth and is reproduced as Chapter 23 of this corpus.

memes.label: Procreation of Horus
memes.description: Isis in the form of a hawk hovers over the body of the dead Osiris and conceives Horus, the divine heir who will avenge his father and restore order.

memes.label: Ptah
memes.description: Creator god of Memphis, patron of craftsmen and architects, who brought all things into being through the thought of his heart and the word of his tongue.

memes.label: Purification
memes.description: The Egyptian practice of ritual purification through water, abstinence, and ceremonial cleansing, required of priests and worshippers before approaching the gods.

memes.label: Ra
memes.description: The supreme sun god of ancient Egypt, self-created creator deity, and king of the gods who ruled over men and gods before retiring to the heavens.

memes.label: Rameses II
memes.description: Rameses II (Ramesses II, "Ra-meses-meri-Amen") is a pharaoh of the XIXth Dynasty during whose reign the Bentresh legend and the Princess of Bekhten narrative are set.

memes.label: Resurrection of Osiris
memes.description: Isis and Nephthys restore Osiris to life through magical spells, making him the ruler of the Underworld and judge of the dead.

memes.label: Sais
memes.description: An ancient city in the Western Nile Delta, cult center of the goddess Neith, site of a statue of Athene identified with Isis, and a venue for the annual festival of Osiris.

memes.label: Isis Searches for Osiris
memes.description: After Set murders Osiris, Isis takes the form of a bird and searches Egypt for her husband's body, eventually finding it at Netat near Abydos with the aid of Nephthys.

memes.label: Seb
memes.description: Earth-god of the Heliopolitan Ennead, son of Shu and Tefnut, father of Osiris, Isis, Set, and Nephthys.

memes.label: Sebau
memes.description: The serpent fiends and demons of darkness who opposed Ra and Horus in Egyptian mythology, allies of Apep and Set in the cosmic struggle against order.

memes.label: Sekhet
memes.description: Lion-headed war goddess of Memphis, consort of Ptah, who brings plague upon the enemies of Ra and healing to those who honour her.

memes.label: Sept
memes.description: Goddess of the star Sothis (Sirius, the Dog-star), whose heliacal rising heralded the annual Nile inundation; associated with Isis and the rebirth of Osiris.

memes.label: Serq
memes.description: Scorpion-goddess of protection and healing, guardian of the dead, who defeated the poison of Apep and protected the infant Horus alongside Isis.

memes.label: Set
memes.description: God of chaos, storms, the desert, and foreign lands; the murderer of Osiris and eternal adversary of Horus.

memes.label: Seven Years' Famine
memes.description: A seven-year famine in the reign of King Tcheser (IIIrd Dynasty), ended through the intercession of the god Khnemu of Elephantine, who appeared to the king in a dream and promised to restore the Nile flood in exchange for the restoration of his temple revenues.
aliases: Famine in the Reign of Tcheser
Legend of Khnemu and the Seven Years' Famine

memes.label: Shu
memes.description: Shu is the primordial god of air, light, and dry atmosphere, who separated the sky (Nut) from the earth (Keb) and upheld the heavens, forming one of the Great Ennead of Heliopolis.

memes.label: Soul
memes.description: The ancient Egyptian concept of the soul as a multi-part entity comprising the Ba, Ka, Khu, and other aspects that together constitute the complete being.

memes.label: Succession of Horus
memes.description: Horus inherits the throne of Egypt by his father Osiris's will, is vindicated in the divine tribunal, and proclaimed the rightful heir, establishing the model of legitimate kingship.

memes.label: Sun Boat
memes.description: The funerary and ritual solar barques — physical models of the celestial Boat of Ra, placed in tombs and temples to enable the deceased king to journey with the sun god through the heavens.

memes.label: Suten-Henen
memes.description: The Egyptian city of Heracleopolis Magna, where Osiris was crowned king and lived, and the site where the slaughter of rebellious mankind began in the Destruction of Mankind legend.

memes.label: Tcheser
memes.description: Tcheser (Djoser) is a king of the IIIrd Dynasty during whose reign, according to Egyptian legend, a seven-year famine afflicted the land and was ended through the intercession of the god Khnemu.

memes.label: Tefnut
memes.description: Tefnut is the Heliopolitan goddess of moisture and the first daughter of Ra, who was emitted from the creator god together with her twin brother Shu, and with him became the mother of Geb and Nut.

memes.label: Tem
memes.description: Tem (Temu, Atum) is the evening form of the Egyptian sun god and a primordial creator deity of Heliopolis, completing the solar triad of Khepera-Ra-Tem.

memes.label: Thebes
memes.description: The ancient Egyptian city of Uast (Greek Thebes), the great southern capital and cult center of Amen-Ra, home to the temples of Luxor and Khensu, famed for its College of Magic.

memes.label: Thoth
memes.description: God of writing, wisdom, magic, and the moon; divine scribe who records the judgment of the dead.

memes.label: Tuat
memes.description: The ancient Egyptian underworld or netherworld through which the sun-god Ra traveled each night, realm of the dead and domain of Osiris.

memes.label: Uatchit
memes.description: Cobra-goddess of Lower Egypt, protector of the pharaoh, and the uraeus-serpent on the royal crown, paired with Nekhebet as the Two Ladies.

memes.label: Uraeus
memes.description: The sacred cobra emblem worn on the crowns of Egyptian gods and pharaohs, symbolizing divine authority, protection, and the fiery Eye of Ra.

memes.label: Winged Disk
memes.description: The Winged Sun Disk — the ancient Egyptian symbol of the god Horus of Behutet, representing divine protection, victory over enemies, and the omnipresence of the sun god.

memes.label: Words of Power
memes.description: The magical utterances (hekau) possessed by the gods and imparted to the deceased, enabling control over the physical and spiritual worlds in Egyptian mythology.
